articles.title: US-Iran nuclear talks stall as Tehran demands release of $24bn in frozen assets
articles.summary: Negotiations to extend the truce and reach a nuclear framework hit an impasse. An adviser to Iran's supreme leader said talks were 'at a deadlock' and that Washington must agree to release roughly $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets, while President Trump insisted negotiations were proceeding at a 'rapid pace' and expected Iran to accept his terms within the week. A confidential IAEA report cited on June 4 urged Tehran to engage 'constructively' over its nuclear material; the US State Department renewed a caution advisory for Americans across the region.

articles.title: Hezbollah rejects Lebanon-Israel ceasefire as the two sides trade fresh strikes
articles.summary: Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected the US-brokered truce announced June 3, demanding 'a comprehensive cessation of aggression, a cease-fire, and the withdrawal of Israel' from Lebanon. Israeli forces and Hezbollah exchanged fire for a second day; an Israeli soldier was killed by an anti-tank missile in southern Lebanon, and a ceasefire-violations tracker logged dozens of Israeli strikes against Hezbollah replies. The IDF ordered residents of at least nine southern towns to evacuate under threat of attack.

articles.title: Lebanese President Aoun accuses Iran of using Lebanon as a 'bargaining chip'
articles.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun publicly criticised Tehran, saying Iran is 'using Lebanon as a bargaining chip' in its negotiations with the United States. His remarks came as Israeli strikes continued across southern Lebanon despite the freshly announced ceasefire and as Beirut sought to distance the Lebanese state from Hezbollah's rejection of the truce.

articles.title: EU approves €100m support package for the Lebanese army
articles.summary: The European Union signed off on a new 100-million-euro ($116 million) support package for the Lebanese armed forces, aiming to bolster the national military amid the fragile ceasefire. The EU said the latest truce between Israel and Lebanon 'offers a chance to prevent a return to full-scale hostilities.'

articles.title: Israel keeps up near-daily Gaza strikes as Hamas refuses to disarm
articles.summary: Israel carried out further strikes across the Gaza Strip, including in Khan Younis, where displaced Palestinians searched destroyed tents. Israel says it is targeting 'imminent' threats; Hamas continues to refuse disarmament. Al Jazeera reports Israel has killed hundreds during the period since the October 2025 ceasefire and expanded its controlled territory, with Netanyahu reiterating a directive to extend control from about 60% to 70% of the enclave.

articles.title: UN warns Middle East conflict is pushing millions toward hunger
articles.summary: United Nations agencies warned that the regional conflict — spanning the Iran war, disruption around the Strait of Hormuz, and fighting in Lebanon and Gaza — is driving a sharp rise in food insecurity, pushing millions of people across the Middle East toward hunger as supply routes and humanitarian access deteriorate.

articles.title: Monitor records surge in Israeli settler attacks on Palestinians in the West Bank
articles.summary: Middle East Monitor reported that documented Israeli settler attacks against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank have surged in 2026, citing more than 870 incidents resulting in casualties or property damage since the start of the year — an average of roughly six attacks per day — amid heightened tensions during the regional conflict.

articles.title: Superdrug pulls Ahava products over links to West Bank settlement
articles.summary: UK retailer Superdrug removed Ahava cosmetics from its shelves after the brand was linked to an Israeli settlement in the occupied West Bank, Arab News reported. The move was welcomed by Palestinian-rights campaigners as part of wider boycott pressure over settlement-linked commerce.

articles.title: Air defence and rocket systems made up 29% of Israel's 2025 defence exports
articles.summary: Al-Monitor reported that rocket and air-defence systems accounted for 29% of Israel's defence exports in 2025, underscoring how the prolonged regional conflict has reshaped demand for Israeli interception and missile technology even as the country fights on multiple fronts.
